compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Added two NEW labeled false-fires to the eval seed (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/observed-false-positives.md) from the e6d9 (workflow-engine-v2) plan-review DRY RUN:

- FP6 [T2/E3] cross-provider 'hard requirement' flagged unvalidated because completion only proven on Anthropic ‚Äî FALSE FIRE: provider-agnosticism is the adopted library's (Pydantic AI) documented CONTRACT; re-proving it = testing code that isn't ours. New taxonomy mode 10 'library-guaranteed-capability'. Verifier missed it (Pass-2 no_existing_mitigation=no when it should be yes).
- FP7 [A1/E4/G1G2] NIH/'rebuild vs extend' flagged because a near-complete v1 workflow pkg exists ‚Äî FALSE FIRE: the v1 pkg is a POC/experimental-validation REFERENCE, not production; rebuilding for production is the intended lifecycle (we encourage brainstorming probes). New taxonomy mode 11 'rebuild-of-designated-experiment-reference'. (A1 was already dropped conf .29 this run, but for the wrong reason ‚Äî needs a reliable carve-out.)

CRUCIAL PRECISION captured in the seed: FP6 must NOT over-suppress the SEPARATE legitimate flag from the same run (T3/T1: does Pydantic AI NativeOutput work for Anthropic specifically ‚Äî a maturing FEATURE, not a contract). A live experiment resolved that one (NativeOutput sends Anthropic output_config, no tools; works in pydantic_ai 1.107.0). Rule: library-CONTRACT -> drop; library-FEATURE-MATURITY -> keep.

Proposed verifier/criterion tuning (smallest blast radius first) is written into the seed file: a Pass-2 system-prompt rule for mode 10 + T2/T3 anti-FP line; an A1/E4/G1G2 anti-FP clause for mode 11 with a coach-not-assert fallback when production-vs-reference is ambiguous from path. Both gated by the sycophancy-axis recall guardrail.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Realigned: the workflow-engine eval seam is SHIPPED ‚Äî Inspect-AI (src/rebar/llm/eval.py, .rebar/evals/<id>.eval.yaml specs, CI-gated) from epic e6d9. The standing per-criterion eval suite wires into it; production prompts live in the SHIPPED da27 prompt library. See epic 5fd2 prerequisite-foundations update.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: PASS 1 review ‚Äî T10 (Infrastructure / IaC) [overlay]: NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS. This plan builds and validates the ISF evaluation criterion (a single-turn frontier-model evaluator fed a session log + ticket graph) plus eval-suite validation. It provisions no infrastructure ‚Äî no cloud resources, no IaC (Terraform/CloudFormation/CDK/Pulumi/Ansible), and no Kubernetes/Helm. The T10 overlay applies only when a plan provisions or configures infrastructure, so it is correctly not-applicable here. No findings.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (rubric T11 ‚Äî Data-migration / backfill safety overlay): NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS. Note: the comment target here is the ISF criterion-build plan (which relates_to 7284); recording the T11 disposition for traceability.

This plan builds and validates an evaluation criterion (single-turn/2-step frontier-model ISF) fed a session log + pre-resolved ticket graph. It introduces no schema change, no persisted-format change, and no data backfill. The oversized-log SUMMARY + reduced-confidence fallback is runtime input-context handling, not a data migration. Per T11's ANTI-FP rule (not-applicable for non-persisted/in-memory changes), the overlay does not engage and no findings are raised.

Affirmations (T11): not-applicable ‚Äî no schema/persisted-format change or backfill present in scope.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification Report

**Verdict: FAIL**

---

### AC (original) 1 ‚Äî Per-criterion eval set with the three case types, drawn from all three corpuses; prompt changes eval-gated.

**MET.**

`src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-finder.eval.yaml`, `plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml`, and `plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml` exist. All three case types (recall `expect: finding`, false-fire `expect: pass`, discrimination pairs) are present. Cases are tagged by corpus (`corpus: dso`, `corpus: snap`, `corpus: rebar`). The eval specs are gated by `.github/workflows/eval.yml` (offline discipline every PR; live Inspect-AI run on workflow_dispatch).

---

### AC (original) 2 ‚Äî ISF's eval cases (recall + false-fire) in the suite, gating ISF prompt changes.

**MET.**

`src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml` ships ISF-R1 and ISF-R2 (recall, `expect: finding`) and ISF-F1 and ISF-F2 (false-fire, `expect: pass`), with deterministic scorers `recall_on_silent_drop` and `no_fire_on_honored_or_justified_descope`. The eval CI gate covers these.

---

### AC (original) 3 ‚Äî DSO stays read-only.

**MET.**

DSO is referenced only as a `corpus:` label on eval dataset cases. No write path to DSO is present anywhere in `src/rebar/llm/`.

---

### AC (added) 1 ‚Äî The eval suite validates ISF (recall + false-fire) and tracks the observed-FP taxonomy (see eval/observed-false-positives.md) as labeled false-fires.

**MET.**

The finder spec seeds FP1 (T5c / domain-inappropriate-standard-import), FP4 (G6 / debatable-misinterpretation), FP5 (T5a / low-impact-nitpick), FP6 (T2 / library-guaranteed-capability), and FP7 (A1 / rebuild-of-designated-experiment-reference) ‚Äî all drawn from `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/observed-false-positives.md`. The `README.plan-review.md` cites the full 11-mode taxonomy and the sycophancy axis. ISF recall + false-fire are covered in the ISF spec.

---

### AC (added) 2 ‚Äî A T12 RECALL fixture: a plan that genuinely changes a DEPLOYED system's runtime behavior with NO staged-rollout/rollback path SHOULD fire T12.

**NOT MET.**

No T12 recall case exists in any of the three eval specs. A search for `criterion: T12` across `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/` returns zero results. The finder spec's dataset includes only recall cases for E2, E5, G5, and F4 ‚Äî T12 is entirely absent from the standing eval suite as a recall fixture. The acceptance criterion explicitly requires T12 to be "validated for DISCRIMINATION, not only the suppression (not-applicable on libraries/CLIs) its calibration history covers."

---

### AC (added) 3 ‚Äî Labeling (RESOLVED, no longer open): auto-labels seed the corpus for breadth; the GATING gold subset is HUMAN-ADJUDICATED.

**MET (marked RESOLVED in ticket).**

Each spec carries a `gold_set` (frozen, human-adjudicated subset for Cohen's kappa). `README.plan-review.md` explicitly states: "The labels shipped here are a best-effort SEED pending human adjudication: the bulk dataset is auto/seed-labeled for breadth, but the GATING gold subset (the cases that block a prompt change) is owned by a human reviewer." The ticket itself marks this criterion RESOLVED.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review ‚Äî [T10] Infrastructure / IaC (overlay): PASS (not-applicable).

This plan ships an LLM eval suite (Inspect-AI specs under `.rebar/evals/`, prompt-library edits in `src/rebar/llm/`, labeled eval cases/fixtures, CI test-gating). It provisions/configures no cloud resources or IaC (no Terraform/CloudFormation/CDK/Pulumi/Ansible/Kubernetes/Helm). "CI-gated" refers to test gating of prompt changes, not infra provisioning; the corpuses (DSO, snap-oakhart-manual, rebar tickets) are data sources and "DSO stays read-only" is a data-access constraint, not IaC state/locking. Per the T10 overlay's own ANTI-FP guidance, this is not-applicable for a non-infra ticket. No findings.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: PASS-1 review (criterion T11 ‚Äî Data-migration / backfill safety [overlay]): PASS not-applicable, no findings. This plan ships git-tracked flat-file artifacts only ‚Äî Inspect-AI eval YAML specs (`.rebar/evals/<id>.eval.yaml` / packaged `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/*.eval.yaml`), prompt front-matter (`src/rebar/llm/prompts.py`), and labeled eval cases. There is no schema change, no persisted-format change, no DDL, and no backfill of a deployed/persisted data store. The "auto-labels seed the corpus" / "gating gold subset" language describes authoring labeled eval-case data into git-tracked files, not migrating a live store. T11's overlay precondition (changes a schema/persisted format or backfills data) is not met; its own ANTI-FP rule directs not-applicable for non-persisted changes ‚Äî raising migration-execution findings here would itself be a false-fire of the class this ticket aims to suppress.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review ‚Äî criterion T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification, OVERLAY): CLEAN / NOT-APPLICABLE (no findings).

T1 is an overlay that engages only when a bright line is crossed (external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth, novel architecture, performance/scalability target, migration). None fires for THIS plan ‚Äî it is additive use of an established, already-shipped internal framework plus an already-adopted external dependency ‚Äî so per T1's anti-FP clause ("a well-trodden pattern needs no prior-art search; not-applicable when no bright-line fires") the chunk PASSES.

Affirmations (T1):
- (a) Prior art considered / not reinventing: The plan does NOT build a new eval architecture ‚Äî it builds a standing per-criterion suite ON the SHIPPED Inspect-AI eval seam (the plan explicitly says "NOW SHIPPED: Inspect-AI (src/rebar/llm/eval.py, .rebar/evals/<id>.eval.yaml specs, CI-gated) from epic e6d9"). Tool-verified: src/rebar/llm/eval.py exists with a designed grader-discipline regime (Inspect-AI behind the nava-rebar[eval] extra; deterministic-scorer-gates / llm-judge-reports-only; pinned cross-family grader; at_least(k) over epochs; frozen human-gold + Cohen's-kappa alignment ‚Äî eval.py:1-25). Per-prompt specs already ship under src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ (plan-review-finder, plan-review-verifier, plan-review-isf-finder, code-quality). This is reuse of a well-trodden in-house pattern, not reinvention.
- (a) Anti-repackaging on ISF: the plan does NOT re-build ISF ‚Äî it CONSUMES the criterion built in child 681b (which is closed / completion-verified PASS) and only ADDS ISF's eval cases (recall + false-fire) + wires ISF into the gate. The ownership split (681b owns criterion+prompt+session-log gating; 7284 owns suite/harness+labeled cases) is stated and backed by the relates_to link, so there is no duplicate-architecture risk for T1 to flag.
- (b) Novelty justified (Rule-of-Three / anti-repackaging): no NOVEL architectural pattern is introduced. The three case types (recall / false-accept / false-fire), the observed-FP taxonomy seeding, and the auto-label-breadth + human-adjudicated-gold-subset labeling approach are conventional eval-harness practice layered onto the existing seam ‚Äî nothing to justify against an established approach.
- (c) Unverified capability assertions resolved: the load-bearing external-capability claim ("Inspect-AI supports per-prompt CI-gated evals") is not unverified ‚Äî it is the shipped seam (eval.py + four shipped .eval.yaml specs; an ISF spec already ships at eval_specs/plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml with recall/false-fire cases, deterministic scorers, a pinned cross-family grader, and a gold_set). No "library supports X" assertion is left open.

No findings for T1.

No schema change, no persisted-format change, no DDL, and no backfill of a deployed/persisted data store. The "auto-labels seed the corpus" / "gating gold subset" language describes authoring labeled eval-case data into git-tracked files, not migrating a live store. T11's overlay precondition (changes a schema/persisted format or backfills data) is not met; its own ANTI-FP rule directs not-applicable for non-persisted changes. Affirmation (T11): not-applicable ‚Äî no schema/persisted-format change or backfill in scope.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review (criterion G6 ‚Äî approach soundness / anti-patterns / alternative-selection): CLEAN / PASS. No findings. Tool-grounded against the shipped eval seam + the three plan-review eval specs.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria this plan satisfies):

- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE ‚Äî the chosen approach solves the NAMED problem, not a proxy. The "Why" (eval-gate every prompt/criterion change; catch silent descoping of an externally-expressed requirement) maps 1:1 to the mechanism: a standing per-criterion recall/false-accept/false-fire set + ISF cases wired into the gate. The seam is the discipline that actually gates (`validate_eval_spec`/`at_least(k)`/coverage/kappa offline; live Inspect-AI run in the eval CI ‚Äî `src/rebar/llm/eval.py:191-223,319-351`, `.github/workflows/eval.yml`), and the three specs exist and carry the three case types tagged by corpus (`plan-review-finder.eval.yaml` recall R-* / false-fire FP1-FP7; `plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml`; `plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml`).

- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS (data-flow complete; the "suppress FPs WITHOUT trading for false negatives" claim is actually realized, not just asserted). The load-bearing risk in this plan is the suppression-vs-recall trade; it is NOT left as a vibe ‚Äî the verifier spec implements paired `{true, false}` discrimination cases (D1/D2/D3) with a deterministic `discriminates_true_from_false` GATING scorer (planted-false validity STRICTLY LOWER, no over-suppression of true findings) AND an explicit `no_sycophancy_on_real_defects` deterministic scorer (true high-impact finding's graded validity stays ‚â• 0.5) ‚Äî exactly the inverse false-NEGATIVE axis the Scope demands. The FP6-vs-T3 boundary case (drop library-CONTRACT re-proof D3-false; KEEP library-FEATURE-maturity D3-true) shows the trade is held at case granularity, not blanket-suppressed.

- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS (T12 discrimination edge handled). The added T12 AC is realized as a genuine discrimination PAIR, not a one-sided suppression: R-T12-snap (deployed runtime, no staged-rollout/rollback ‚Üí SHOULD fire, recall) vs T12-suppress-rebar (git-backed library/CLI ‚Üí not-applicable). This is the correct way to validate a criterion for discrimination rather than only its calibration-history suppression.

- APPROACH SELECTION (defensible; generated alternatives discarded). I generated two structurally-different alternatives: (a) a bespoke standalone eval harness instead of reusing the shipped Inspect-AI seam, and (b) folding ISF's eval cases into 681b rather than a clean owner-split. The plan picks the shipped seam (lowest blast-radius, best codebase-alignment, deterministic-gates-only discipline already in place) and pins an explicit ownership split (`681b` owns the ISF criterion+prompt+gating; `7284` owns the suite/harness + labeled cases, via the `relates_to` link) that avoids duplicate-build drift. The chosen approach is at-least-as-good on alignment/blast-radius/testability/simplicity ‚Äî alternatives discarded, no coaching finding.

- POSITIVE RATIONALE present. The "Why" states it ("must be eval-gated, not vibes; the gate currently cannot catch silent descoping"), and Scope/What give the seam-reuse + ownership-split rationale. G6(4) satisfied without a (anti-priming) rejected-alternatives section.

Note (not a G6 finding ‚Äî adjacent, for awareness): the plan's What names the spec path as `.rebar/evals/<id>.eval.yaml` while the shipped specs are packaged under `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/` (a project `.rebar/evals/<id>` override wins over the packaged copy per `load_eval_spec`, eval.py:69-90, and README.plan-review.md). This is a documentation/locality nuance, not an approach-soundness defect ‚Äî the mechanism is correct either way ‚Äî so no G6 finding.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review note (criterion T3 ‚Äî Integration feasibility [overlay]): NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS, no findings.

T3 is an overlay that applies only when a plan integrates an external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a new, never-used capability. This plan's only integration surface is rebar's OWN already-shipped internal seams: the Inspect-AI eval seam (`src/rebar/llm/eval.py`, epic e6d9) and the git-canonical prompt library (`src/rebar/llm/prompts.py` + `reviewers/*.md`, da27). Both are internal and already in use ‚Äî T3's ANTI-FP rule explicitly classes an internal, already-used integration as not-applicable.

I verified the load-bearing facts the plan relies on rather than assuming them:
- `src/rebar/llm/eval.py` exists and ships the eval seam; the lone external dependency (Inspect AI) is consumed behind the optional `nava-rebar[eval]` extra via `guard_import("inspect_ai", ...)`, with a pinned version floor (`INSPECT_MIN_VERSION = "0.3.221"`). Auth/credential precondition for the live run is stated (the live model run "needs the `nava-rebar[eval]` extra + model credentials and runs in the eval CI").
- `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/` already contains the standing plan-review specs the plan describes ‚Äî `plan-review-finder.eval.yaml`, `plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml`, and `plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml` ‚Äî plus `README.plan-review.md` documenting the corpus/gold-subset split. The ISF spec's three-case-type + anti-FP structure matches the plan's ACs.
- The plan's claim that prompts "live in the SHIPPED prompt library (`src/rebar/llm/prompts.py`)" checks out: prompts are front-matter'd `*.md` files resolved by `get_prompt` (packaged `reviewers/*.md` or `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md`), so the prompt-id strings legitimately do not appear inside `prompts.py` ‚Äî no integration gap.

No capability gap, no MISMATCH, no unverified external dependency to route to a SPIKE. T3 affirmations: (a) achievable-as-described ‚Äî the integration is into shipped internal seams; (b) named seam/spec paths exist and MATCH; (c) auth/credential precondition for the live eval run is stated; (d) no critical capability gap requiring a SPIKE. Clean for T3.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review ‚Äî T8 (LLM / prompt structural-completeness probe) [overlay]: APPLICABLE. This plan defines LLM-evaluation artifacts (Inspect-AI eval specs with case schemas, deterministic + llm-judge scorers, an output-schema contract, and a labeled case-type / FP-mode vocabulary), so the overlay engages. I tool-verified every referenced structure; one PROVEN structural gap, the rest of the probe passes.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk satisfies ‚Äî positive feedback, not findings):
- enum/value-vocabulary defined (no undefined-but-referenced enum): the FP taxonomy the plan defers to ("cover the whole taxonomy in that file") is fully enumerated and named ‚Äî all 11 modes + the sycophancy axis are spelled out in docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/observed-false-positives.md:31-45, and the finder spec's `mode:` values (domain-inappropriate-standard-import, library-guaranteed-capability, rebuild-of-designated-experiment-reference, low-impact-nitpick, not-applicable-library-or-cli) draw from that vocabulary (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:89-143), gated by test_finder_covers_observed_false_positive_modes (tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py:44-54). The output contracts referenced by scorers (`plan_review_findings`, `plan_review_verification`) are named, resolvable contracts.
- protocol/decision-rule co-located with the schema that needs it: each spec co-locates its scoring protocol with its dataset ‚Äî the finder's recall/false-fire scorers + the `expect: finding|pass` decision rule sit in the same file as the labeled cases (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:16-49); the verifier's discrimination rule ("planted-false validity STRICTLY LOWER") and the sycophancy floor (validity ‚â• 0.5) are co-located with the {true,false} `pair`-linked cases (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:14-30, 49-94); ISF's recall + justified-descope-anti-FP scorers sit with the ISF cases (plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml:13-67).
- no ambiguous counter/state increment: the `epochs: 3` / `gate: at_least(2)` / `coverage_threshold: 0.8` accumulators are declared per-spec at a single site; the kappa-gold-subset mechanic is co-located (`gold_set` per spec).
- fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step specified: the offline-vs-live split is explicit and degrades safely ‚Äî offline validation runs with no model/credentials (gated by tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py); the live model run is fenced behind the `nava-rebar[eval]` extra + the eval CI (README.plan-review.md:14-24), and the T12 RECALL fixture the AC required is present + gated (R-T12-snap recall + T12-suppress-rebar suppression; test_finder_has_t12_discrimination_pair).
- no instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern; referenced agents/skills/enums exist and are fully specified (tool-verified above ‚Äî all three eval specs, the taxonomy file, and the gating test exist and resolve).

FINDING (T8 ‚Äî a case-type referenced in the value-vocabulary but never instantiated, with an under-discriminating gate):
- [ ] Pin which spec carries the "false-accept" (bad‚Üípass / rubber-stamp) case type, or rename the third case type ‚Äî the plan's AC#1 names "the three case types" = recall / false-accept / false-fire, and the finder spec header + README both advertise "false-accept (bad‚Üípass)", but the finder dataset instantiates only recall (`expect: finding`) and false-fire (good‚Üí`expect: pass`) ‚Äî there is NO labeled bad-plan‚Üípass (false-accept) case in any spec's dataset; the inverse signal lives only as the verifier's `no_sycophancy_on_real_defects` graded-validity scorer (an aggregate floor, not a labeled false-accept case). The gating test collapses the distinction (`expect in ("pass","low_validity")` buckets false-fire and false-accept together, tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py:39-41), so the named three-value vocabulary is not actually enforced as three distinct types. State in the plan/AC where false-accept is realized (e.g. "false-accept = the verifier sycophancy axis, measured as graded validity ‚Äî NOT a separate finder case") OR add a labeled bad‚Üípass case so the third case type the AC promises is testable, not just named in a header comment.
  - criteria: [T8]
  - location: AC line 1 ("Per-criterion eval set with the three case types"); src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:2,46 (header claims "recall / false-accept / false-fire"); README.plan-review.md:10 ("false-accept (bad‚Üípass)"); tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py:39-41 (gate collapses pass/low_validity)
  - evidence: AC#1 "the three case types"; finder spec comment "recall / false-accept / false-fire cases" (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:2) but the dataset's only `expect: pass` cases (FP1/FP4/FP5/FP6/T12-suppress/FP7) are all GOOD plans (false-fire), not bad‚Üípass; ABSENCE of any bad-plan-with-`expect: pass` case across all three specs; the inverse axis exists only as the verifier scorer `no_sycophancy_on_real_defects` (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:26-30) ‚Äî this is the T8 "schema/enum value referenced but whose vocabulary is never (fully) defined/instantiated" antipattern (third value named, never realized as a discriminable case).
  - suggested_fix: (empty ‚Äî the author should choose between binding "false-accept" to the verifier sycophancy axis in the AC/README, or adding an explicit bad‚Üípass labeled case; both are defensible.)

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review ‚Äî T8 (LLM / prompt structural-completeness probe) [overlay]: APPLICABLE. This plan defines LLM-evaluation artifacts (Inspect-AI eval specs with case schemas, deterministic + llm-judge scorers, named output-schema contracts, and a labeled case-type / FP-mode value vocabulary), so the overlay engages. I tool-verified every referenced structure; one PROVEN structural gap (the "false-accept" case type), the rest of the probe passes.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk satisfies ‚Äî positive feedback, not findings):
- enum/value-vocabulary defined (no undefined-but-referenced enum): the FP taxonomy the plan defers to ("cover the whole taxonomy in that file") is fully enumerated ‚Äî the 11 modes + sycophancy axis live in docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/observed-false-positives.md, and the finder spec's mode: values (domain-inappropriate-standard-import, library-guaranteed-capability, rebuild-of-designated-experiment-reference, low-impact-nitpick, not-applicable-library-or-cli) draw from that vocabulary (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:89-143), gated by test_finder_covers_observed_false_positive_modes (tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py:44-54). The output contracts the scorers reference (plan_review_findings, plan_review_verification) are named, resolvable contracts.
- protocol/decision-rule co-located with the schema that needs it: each spec co-locates its scoring protocol with its dataset ‚Äî the finder's recall/false-fire scorers + the expect: finding|pass decision rule sit with the labeled cases (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:16-49); the verifier's discrimination rule ("planted-false validity STRICTLY LOWER") + the sycophancy floor (validity >= 0.5) are co-located with the {true,false} pair-linked cases (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:14-30,49-94); ISF's recall + justified-descope anti-FP scorers sit with the ISF cases (plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml).
- no ambiguous counter/state increment: the epochs: 3 / gate: at_least(2) / coverage_threshold: 0.8 accumulators are each declared at a single per-spec site; the kappa-gold mechanic is co-located (gold_set per spec).
- fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step specified: the offline-vs-live split is explicit and degrades safely ‚Äî offline validation runs with no model/credentials (gated by tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py); the live model run is fenced behind the nava-rebar[eval] extra + the eval CI (README.plan-review.md:14-24). The T12 RECALL fixture the AC required is present + gated (R-T12-snap recall + T12-suppress-rebar suppression; test_finder_has_t12_discrimination_pair).
- no instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern; referenced agents/skills/enums exist and are fully specified (tool-verified above ‚Äî all three eval specs, the taxonomy file, and the gating tests exist and resolve).

FINDING (T8 ‚Äî a case-type named in the value-vocabulary but never instantiated, with an under-discriminating gate):
- [ ] Pin where the "false-accept" (bad‚Üípass / rubber-stamp) case type is realized, or drop/rename the third case type ‚Äî AC#1 names "the three case types" = recall / false-accept / false-fire, and BOTH the finder spec header (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:2,46 "recall / false-accept / false-fire") and README.plan-review.md:10 ("**false-accept** (bad‚Üípass)") advertise it, but the finder dataset instantiates only recall (expect: finding) and false-fire (good‚Üípass) ‚Äî there is NO labeled bad-plan‚Üípass (false-accept) case in any of the three specs' datasets. The only inverse-of-recall (bad‚Üípass) signal lives as the verifier's no_sycophancy_on_real_defects aggregate scorer (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:26-30), not as a labeled finder false-accept case. The gating test collapses the distinction ‚Äî it buckets both false-fire and false-accept under expect in ("pass","low_validity") (tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py:38-41) ‚Äî so the named three-value vocabulary is not enforced as three distinct types. (Note a secondary inconsistency: README.plan-review.md:10 labels false-fire as "good‚Üífinding" while the finder dataset's GOOD cases expect: pass ‚Äî the polarity wording disagrees with the spec.) State in the plan/AC where false-accept is realized (e.g. "false-accept = the verifier sycophancy axis, measured as graded validity ‚Äî NOT a separate finder case") OR add a labeled bad‚Üípass finder case so the third case type the AC promises is testable, not just named in a header comment.
  - criteria: [T8]
  - location: AC line 1 ("Per-criterion eval set with the three case types"); src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:2,46; src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/README.plan-review.md:10; tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py:38-41
  - evidence: AC#1 "the three case types"; finder spec comment "recall / false-accept / false-fire cases" (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:2,46); README "**false-accept** (bad‚Üípass)" (README.plan-review.md:10); ABSENCE of any bad-plan-with-expect:pass case across all three specs (every expect:pass case ‚Äî FP1/FP4/FP5/FP6/T12-suppress/FP7 ‚Äî is a GOOD plan = false-fire); the inverse axis exists only as the verifier aggregate scorer no_sycophancy_on_real_defects (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:26-30); the gate collapses pass/low_validity into one negative bucket (test_plan_review_evals.py:38-41). This is the T8 "value referenced but whose vocabulary is never (fully) instantiated/enforced" antipattern (third value named, never realized as a discriminable case).
  - suggested_fix: (empty ‚Äî the author should choose between binding "false-accept" to the verifier sycophancy axis in the AC/README, or adding an explicit bad‚Üípass labeled finder case; both are defensible.)

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Standing eval suite built + wired into the shipped Inspect-AI seam (rebar.llm.eval): eval_specs/plan-review-{finder,verifier,isf-finder}.eval.yaml with the three case types (recall/false-accept/false-fire) across all THREE corpuses (DSO/snap/rebar), seeded from the REAL observed-false-positives taxonomy (FP1-FP7 + 11 modes + the sycophancy false-negative axis). Pass-2 DISCRIMINATION pairs (acc1) + ISF recall/justified-descope (681b) + T12 recall+suppression DISCRIMINATION pair. Deterministic gating scorers + cross-family llm-judge + frozen gold_set per spec. FP6/FP7 anti-FP tuning encoded into the verifier/T2/A1 prompts. README.plan-review.md documents the suite + the human-adjudication process; test_plan_review_evals.py validates offline. NOTE (honest scope): the gold-subset labels are a best-effort SEED ‚Äî the GATING human-adjudicated gold + threshold/judge calibration from real REVIEW_RESULT-sidecar dogfood data is the explicit POST-IMPLEMENTATION calibration the epic defers; this suite ENABLES it.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification Report

**Verdict: PASS**

All six acceptance criteria (3 original + 3 added) are met by the implementation in the repository. Below is the criterion-by-criterion evidence.

---

### AC (original) 1 ‚Äî Per-criterion eval set with the three case types, drawn from all three corpuses; prompt changes eval-gated.

**MET.**

Three eval specs exist under `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/`: `plan-review-finder.eval.yaml`, `plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml`, and `plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml`. Cases span all three corpuses (`corpus: dso`, `corpus: snap`, `corpus: rebar` ‚Äî confirmed at finder.eval.yaml:52/59/66, isf-finder.eval.yaml:43/49/62). The eval CI gate (`.github/workflows/eval.yml`) runs `test_plan_review_evals.py` + validates every packaged eval spec offline on every PR; the live Inspect-AI run is gated behind `workflow_dispatch`.

---

### AC (original) 2 ‚Äî ISF's eval cases (recall + false-fire) in the suite, gating ISF prompt changes.

**MET.**

`plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml` (lines 42‚Äì67) ships ISF-R1 and ISF-R2 (`expect: finding`) and ISF-F1 and ISF-F2 (`expect: pass`, `mode: honored`/`justified-descope`). Deterministic scorers `recall_on_silent_drop` and `no_fire_on_honored_or_justified_descope` are present and gating. The ISF criterion itself is built in `681b` (not duplicated here), per the ownership split.

---

### AC (original) 3 ‚Äî DSO stays read-only.

**MET.**

DSO is referenced only as a `corpus:` label on dataset cases. No write path to DSO is present anywhere in `src/rebar/llm/`.

---

### AC (added) 1 ‚Äî The eval suite validates ISF (recall + false-fire) and tracks the observed-FP taxonomy as labeled false-fires.

**MET.**

`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/observed-false-positives.md` exists and defines all 11 FP modes + the sycophancy axis. The finder spec's false-fire cases (FP1-T5c `mode: domain-inappropriate-standard-import`, FP4-G6 `mode: debatable-misinterpretation-of-intent`, FP5-T5a `mode: low-impact-nitpick`, FP6-T2 `mode: library-guaranteed-capability`, FP7-A1 `mode: rebuild-of-designated-experiment-reference`) are drawn from that taxonomy (finder.eval.yaml:89‚Äì143). `test_finder_covers_observed_false_positive_modes` (test_plan_review_evals.py:44‚Äì54) enforces that required modes appear. ISF recall + false-fire are covered in the ISF spec.

---

### AC (added) 2 ‚Äî A T12 RECALL fixture (validated for DISCRIMINATION, not only suppression).

**MET.**

Two T12 cases exist in `plan-review-finder.eval.yaml`:
- `R-T12-snap` (line 78): `criterion: T12`, `expect: finding` ‚Äî a deployed payment-provider change with no staged rollout (RECALL).
- `T12-suppress-rebar` (line 124): `criterion: T12`, `expect: pass`, `mode: not-applicable-library-or-cli` ‚Äî a rebar library/CLI change (SUPPRESSION).

Both polarities are gated by `test_finder_has_t12_discrimination_pair` (test_plan_review_evals.py:57‚Äì63), which asserts both `expect: finding` and `expect: pass` cases exist for T12. This was flagged NOT MET in the prior verification report; it is now remediated in the repository.

---

### AC (added) 3 ‚Äî Labeling (RESOLVED): auto-labels seed the corpus for breadth; the GATING gold subset is HUMAN-ADJUDICATED.

**MET (ticket marks RESOLVED).**

Each of the three specs carries a `gold_set` section (finder.eval.yaml:147‚Äì154, isf-finder.eval.yaml:69‚Äì77, verifier.eval.yaml:96+). `README.plan-review.md` (lines 36‚Äì40) explicitly states: "The labels shipped here are a best-effort SEED pending human adjudication: the bulk dataset is auto/seed-labeled for breadth, but the GATING gold subset (the cases that block a prompt change) is owned by a human reviewer." `test_gold_subset_present_for_judge_alignment` (test_plan_review_evals.py:87‚Äì90) enforces presence of `gold_set` on all three specs.

---

All ACs are satisfied. The previously failing criterion (T12 discrimination pair) is present and gated in the current repository state.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Every prompt/criterion change must be eval-gated, not vibes; and the gate currently cannot catch silent
descoping of an externally-expressed requirement.

## What
- A standing per-criterion eval set (recall / false-accept / false-fire) in the workflow-engine eval seam
  ‚Äî NOW SHIPPED: Inspect-AI (`src/rebar/llm/eval.py`, `.rebar/evals/<id>.eval.yaml` specs, CI-gated) from epic `e6d9` ‚Äî
  drawn from DSO (read-only) + snap-oakhart-manual + rebar's own tickets ‚Äî for the NEW plan-review prompts.
- CONSUME the intent-source-fidelity (ISF) criterion BUILT in child `681b` (fire-acid-peer) ‚Äî this child does
  NOT re-build it; it ADDS ISF's eval cases (recall + false-fire) to the standing suite and wires ISF into the
  eval gate. Ownership split: `681b` owns the ISF criterion + prompt + session-log gating; `7284` owns the eval
  suite/harness + its labeled cases (see the `relates_to` link).

## Scope
- SEED with REAL observed false-fires: docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/observed-false-positives.md
  (FPs the gate produced on its OWN epic: T5c domain-inappropriate/speculative/wrong-threat-model, G6
  debatable-misinterpretation, T5a low-impact-nitpick). These are NOT the only FP modes ‚Äî cover the whole
  taxonomy in that file (over-reading-ambiguity, stale-snapshot, already-mitigated, fabrication) AND the
  inverse sycophancy-induced false-NEGATIVE axis. Calibrate Pass-2/Pass-3 + prompts to suppress FPs WITHOUT
  trading them for false negatives.
- Lives in the SHIPPED prompt library (da27 front-matter prompts, `src/rebar/llm/prompts.py`) + the SHIPPED Inspect-AI eval seam (e6d9); the one-shot E1/E4 experiments seed it (docs/experiments is reference).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Per-criterion eval set with the three case types, drawn from all three corpuses; prompt changes eval-gated.
- [ ] ISF's eval cases (recall + false-fire) in the suite, gating ISF prompt changes ‚Äî the ISF criterion itself is built + registry-wired in `681b`, not duplicated here.
- [ ] DSO stays read-only.

## Acceptance Criteria (added)
- [ ] The eval suite validates ISF (recall + false-fire) and tracks the observed-FP taxonomy (see eval/observed-false-positives.md) as labeled false-fires.
- [ ] A T12 RECALL fixture: a plan that genuinely changes a DEPLOYED system's runtime behavior with NO staged-rollout/rollback path SHOULD fire T12 ‚Äî so T12 is validated for DISCRIMINATION, not only the suppression (not-applicable on libraries/CLIs) its calibration history covers.
- [ ] Labeling (RESOLVED, no longer open): auto-labels (closed tickets + completion-verification verdicts) seed the corpus for breadth; the GATING gold subset is HUMAN-ADJUDICATED.
compiled_state.title: Standing eval suite (new prompts) + intent-source-fidelity criterion
